audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's challenge to retribution theology: hidden times, social injustice, exploitation of poor/widows/orphans, night crimes, divine delay, and unresolved wicked prosperity.

verses.restored: Why are times not stored up by Shaddai, and why do those who know him not see his days?
People move boundary markers; they seize flocks and pasture them.
They drive away the donkey of orphans; they take the widow’s ox as pledge.
They turn the needy aside from the way; the poor of the earth hide together.
Look, like wild donkeys in the wilderness, they go out to their work, seeking food; the desert gives bread for their young.
In the field they harvest fodder, and they glean the vineyard of the wicked.
They spend the night naked, without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.
They are wet with mountain rain and cling to the rock for lack of shelter.
The orphan is torn from the breast, and a pledge is taken from the poor.
They go about naked, without clothing; hungry, they carry sheaves.
Between their walls they press oil; they tread winepresses, yet thirst.
From the city people groan, and the soul of the wounded cries out; yet the Divine does not charge it as folly.
They are among rebels against light; they do not recognize its ways and do not remain in its paths.
At dawn the murderer rises; he kills the poor and needy, and in the night he is like a thief.
The eye of the adulterer waits for twilight, saying, No eye will see me, and he sets a covering on his face.
In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves in; they do not know light.
For deep darkness is morning to all of them, because they know the terrors of deep darkness.
They are swift on the face of the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth; no one turns toward the way of the vineyards.
Drought and heat steal snow-waters; Sheol steals those who have sinned.
The womb forgets him; worms feed sweetly on him; he is remembered no more, and injustice is broken like a tree.
He feeds on the barren woman who does not bear and does no good to the widow.
Yet he draws the mighty by his power; he rises up, and no one is sure of life.
He gives him security, and he leans on it; yet his eyes are on their ways.
They are lifted a little, then are no more; they are brought low, gathered like all others, and cut off like heads of grain.
If it is not so, who will make me a liar and make my word nothing?
